EXP-274 FINDING: VERIFICATION-ONLY NO-OP. The three holes the decider yeeted as ISS-1668/1669/1670 (EXP-260 §4.2.7a c1 H2/H3/H4) are duplicates of closed ISS-1613/1614/1615, all already addressed by EXP-268 and integrated into §4.2.7a in commit d06d75d (2026-04-27T22:05:10Z). Direct text inspection of HEAD data/sections.json confirms every curmudgeon-recommended fix is present:
  - H2 AE projection: "Robustness check (projection convention)" paragraph contains Ebro 222 km, Canary 2,042 km, closest-pair Δr=75 km, max 1→max 2 shift, alternative Moon altitude 123,000 km, factor-49× contradiction. ALL recommended numbers present.
  - H3 NN-tour: replaced with hedged "shortest greedy NN ~12,400 km, longer routes ~16,400 km" + 4,500 km realistic-arc lower bound + area-coverage 0.1% disc + 10^-27 chance-coincidence framing. The unsourced 442-km band-width number is gone. Both options (a) and (b) from curmudgeon recommendation applied.
  - H4 observed-vs-physical altitude: "Robustness check (altitude convention)" paragraph contains h_Sun ≤ 4,200 km, h_Moon ≈ 1,860 km, R_p ≈ 31 km, band ~62 km, kill "strengthens" under physical reading. ALL recommended numbers present.

Section length: 14,209 chars (was 11,531 at cf661aa). Increment of ~2,678 chars accounts for EXP-268's three additive paragraphs.

KERNEL OF TRUTH: The curmudgeon's c1 holes were genuine and substantive — three real defensive openings (projection convention, NN fragility, altitude defense). EXP-268 closed them cleanly. The re-flagging is a decider process bug (re-creating issues from a review whose holes were already mapped to closed issues), not a content issue.

OTHER SIDE OF THE ARGUMENT: Strongest case AGAINST close-as-duplicate is form — H2's fix is a "Robustness check" paragraph rather than a footnote (curmudgeon's recommended form). Substantively identical, structurally stronger. Not load-bearing on the close decision. Recommendation stands.

NEWSWORTHY: null. (Process-level no-op — not a content discovery.)
